title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: May 25, 2026: US Markets Closed for Memorial Day

items.headline: Global Markets Rally on US-Iran Deal Hopes, Oil Prices Fall; US Futures Higher Ahead of Holiday
items.summary: While US cash markets are closed today for Memorial Day, global investor sentiment is largely positive. US stock futures saw gains, driven by optimism around a potential US-Iran peace agreement, which has significantly pushed crude oil prices lower. Asian and European markets also posted strong performances.

items.statContext: US cash market closed. S&P 500 posted its eighth straight weekly gain last week, rising 0.88%.
US cash market closed. Nasdaq 100 gained 1.22% last week, aided by semiconductor stocks.
US cash market closed. Dow Jones Industrial Average surged 2.13% last week to a record high.
Closed up 1.32% on May 25, extending gains for a second session.
Closed up 1.42% on May 25, surging over 1,000 points.

items.summary: Shares of enGene Therapeutics Inc. have seen a significant decline this month.

items.headline: GD Culture Group Limited drops amid going-private proposal
items.summary: GD Culture Group Limited experienced a sharp drop in May following news of a preliminary non-binding going-private proposal.

items.summary: P3 Health Partners Inc. has been a top performer so far in May.

items.headline: Hyliion Holdings Corp. sees strong monthly surge
items.summary: Hyliion Holdings Corp. recorded significant gains this month.

items.headline: Agilysys Inc. among top monthly performers
items.summary: Agilysys Inc. has shown strong performance throughout May.

items.headline: Crude Oil Prices Plunge Below $100 on US-Iran Deal Optimism
items.summary: Brent crude slipped below the $100-per-barrel mark, falling more than 5% as signs of progress emerged in negotiations between the United States and Iran over reopening the Strait of Hormuz. This decline is boosting global investor sentiment.

items.headline: Fed Officials Hint at Potential Rate Hikes, Signal Shift Away from Easing Bias
items.summary: Fed Governor Christopher Waller indicated that inflation risks suggest the Federal Reserve should no longer signal rate cuts and cannot rule out future hikes. Minutes from the April FOMC meeting reveal a growing desire among participants to remove an easing bias, making rate hikes more likely if inflation persists above 2%. The odds of a Fed rate hike in 2026 have surged to 37%.

items.headline: AI Infrastructure Boom Continues to Drive Tech Sector
items.summary: The artificial intelligence infrastructure boom remains a primary engine for market enthusiasm, with significant deployment of AI infrastructure across enterprises, cloud providers, and consumer applications. Leading semiconductor companies like Nvidia and AMD report strong demand for AI accelerators.

items.headline: Iran Conflict Causes Widespread Supply Chain Disruptions
items.summary: The ongoing Iran conflict and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz are causing significant supply chain disruptions across chemicals, technology, manufacturing, and auto sectors. Analysts warn of rising input costs and shortages of key products.

items.headline: $WMT Shares Fall Post-Earnings Despite EPS Beat
items.summary: Walmart Inc. shares declined 9.8% in the week following its first-quarter results. The company reported revenues broadly in line with analyst forecasts, but statutory earnings per share (EPS) beat expectations by 2.7%.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases Expected This Week
items.summary: Investors will be watching for the second estimate of Q1-2026 GDP and April's core PCE inflation gauge, both due on Thursday. May's Consumer Confidence Index is also slated for release on Tuesday.

items.headline: Eicher Motors Reports Strong Quarterly Earnings
items.summary: Indian automaker Eicher Motors saw its shares climb following better-than-expected quarterly earnings, driven by robust Royal Enfield sales.
